name: packageurl-python

metadata.name: packageurl-python
metadata.summary: A purl aka. Package URL parser and builder

documentation:
---
substitutions:
  ci-tests: |-
    ```{image} https://github.com/package-url/packageurl-python/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg?branch=main
    :alt: CI Tests and build status
    :target: https://github.com/package-url/packageurl-python/actions/workflows/ci.yml
    ```
---

# packageurl-python

Python library to parse and build "purl" aka. Package URLs.
See <https://github.com/package-url/purl-spec> for details.

Join the discussion at <https://gitter.im/package-url/Lobby> or enter a ticket for support.

License: MIT

## Tests and build status

| **Tests and build** |
| ------------------- |
| {{ ci-tests }}      |

## Install

```
pip install packageurl-python
```

## Usage

```
>>> from packageurl import PackageURL

>>> purl = PackageURL.from_string("pkg:maven/org.apache.commons/io@1.3.4")
>>> print(purl.to_dict())
{'type': 'maven', 'namespace': 'org.apache.commons', 'name': 'io', 'version': '1.3.4', 'qualifiers': None, 'subpath': None}

>>> print(purl.to_string())
pkg:maven/org.apache.commons/io@1.3.4

>>> print(str(purl))
pkg:maven/org.apache.commons/io@1.3.4

>>> print(repr(purl))
PackageURL(type='maven', namespace='org.apache.commons', name='io', version='1.3.4', qualifiers={}, subpath=None)
```

## Utilities

### Django models

`packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLMixin` is a Django abstract model mixin to
use Package URLs in Django.

### SQLAlchemy mixin

`packageurl.contrib.sqlalchemy.mixin.PackageURLMixin` is a SQLAlchemy declarative mixin
to use Package URLs in SQLAlchemy models.

### URL to PURL

`packageurl.contrib.url2purl.get_purl(url)` returns a Package URL inferred from an URL.

```
>>> from packageurl.contrib import url2purl
>>> url2purl.get_purl("https://github.com/package-url/packageurl-python")
PackageURL(type='github', namespace='package-url', name='packageurl-python', version=None, qualifiers={}, subpath=None)
```

### PURL to URL

- `packageurl.contrib.purl2url.get_repo_url(purl)` returns a repository URL inferred
  from a Package URL.
- `packageurl.contrib.purl2url.get_download_url(purl)` returns a download URL inferred
  from a Package URL.
- `packageurl.contrib.purl2url.get_inferred_urls(purl)` return all inferred URLs
  (repository, download) from a Package URL.

```
>>> from packageurl.contrib import purl2url

>>> purl2url.get_repo_url("pkg:gem/bundler@2.3.23")
"https://rubygems.org/gems/bundler/versions/2.3.23"

>>> purl2url.get_download_url("pkg:gem/bundler@2.3.23")
"https://rubygems.org/downloads/bundler-2.3.23.gem"

>>> purl2url.get_inferred_urls("pkg:gem/bundler@2.3.23")
["https://rubygems.org/gems/bundler/versions/2.3.23", "https://rubygems.org/downloads/bundler-2.3.23.gem"]
```

## Run tests

Install test dependencies:

```
python3 thirdparty/virtualenv.pyz --never-download --no-periodic-update .
bin/pip install -e ."[test]"
```

Run tests:

```
bin/pytest tests
```

## Make a new release

- Start a new release branch

- Update the CHANGELOG.rst, AUTHORS.rst, and README.rst if needed

- Bump version in setup.cfg

- Run all tests

- Install restview and validate that all .rst docs are correct

- Commit and push this branch

- Make a PR and merge once approved

- Tag and push that tag. This triggers the pypi-release.yml workflow that takes care of
  building the dist release files and upload those to pypi:

  ```
  VERSION=v0.x.x
  git tag -a $VERSION -m "Tag $VERSION"
  git push origin $VERSION
  ```

- Review the GitHub release created by the workflow at
  <https://github.com/package-url/packageurl-python/releases>

functions.name: packageurl.utils.get_golang_purl
documentation:
Return a PackageURL object given an imported `go_package`
or go module "name version" string as seen in a go.mod file.
\>>> get_golang_purl(go_package="github.com/gorilla/mux v1.8.1")
PackageURL(type='golang', namespace='github.com/gorilla', name='mux', version='v1.8.1', qualifiers={}, subpath=None)

functions.name: packageurl.contrib.django.utils.purl_to_lookups
documentation:
Return a lookups dictionary built from the provided `purl` (Package URL) string.
These lookups can be used as QuerySet filters.
If include_empty_fields is provided, the resulting dictionary will include fields
with empty values. This is useful to get exact match.
Note that empty values are always returned as empty strings as the model fields
are defined with `blank=True` and `null=False`.

functions.name: packageurl.contrib.django.utils.without_empty_values
documentation:
Return a new dict not including empty value entries from `input_dict`.

`None`, empty string, empty list, and empty dict/set are cleaned.
`0` and `False` values are kept.

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLQuerySetMixin

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLQuerySetMixin.for_package_url
documentation:
Filter the QuerySet based on a Package URL (purl) string with an option for
exact match filtering.

When `exact_match` is False (default), the method will match any purl with the
same base fields as `purl_str` and allow variations in other fields.
When `exact_match` is True, only the identical purl will be returned.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLQuerySetMixin.with_package_url
documentation: Return objects with Package URL defined.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLQuerySetMixin.without_package_url
documentation: Return objects with empty Package URL.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLQuerySetMixin.empty_package_url
documentation: Return objects with empty Package URL. Alias of without_package_url.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLQuerySetMixin.order_by_package_url
documentation: Order by Package URL fields.
Add Package URL filtering methods to a django.db.models.QuerySet.

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLMixin

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLMixin.package_url
documentation: Return the Package URL "purl" string.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLMixin.get_package_url
documentation: Get the PackageURL instance.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.django.models.PackageURLMixin.set_package_url
documentation:
Set each field values to the values of the provided `package_url` string
or PackageURL object.
Existing values are always overwritten, forcing the new value or an
empty string on all the `package_url` fields since we do not want to
keep any previous values.
Abstract Model for Package URL "purl" fields support.

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.django.filters.PackageURLFilter
documentation:
Filter by an exact Package URL string.

The special "EMPTY" value allows retrieval of objects with an empty Package URL.

This filter depends on `for_package_url` and `empty_package_url`
methods to be available on the Model Manager,
see for example `PackageURLQuerySetMixin`.

When exact_match_only is True, the filter will match only exact Package URL strings.

functions.name: packageurl.contrib.url2purl.build_bitbucket_purl

Return a PackageURL object from BitBucket `url`.
For example:
https://bitbucket.org/TG1999/first_repo/src/master or
https://bitbucket.org/TG1999/first_repo/src or
https://bitbucket.org/TG1999/first_repo/src/master/new_folder

functions.name: packageurl.contrib.url2purl.build_gitlab_purl

For example:
https://gitlab.com/TG1999/firebase/-/tree/1a122122/views
https://gitlab.com/TG1999/firebase/-/tree
https://gitlab.com/TG1999/firebase/-/master

functions.name: packageurl.quote
documentation:
Return a percent-encoded unicode string, except for colon :, given an `s`
byte or unicode string.

functions.name: packageurl.unquote
documentation:
Return a percent-decoded unicode string, given an `s` byte or unicode
string.

functions.name: packageurl.get_quoter
documentation: Return quoting callable given an `encode` tri-boolean (True, False or None)

functions.name: packageurl.normalize_qualifiers
documentation:
Return normalized `qualifiers` as a mapping (or as a string if `encode` is
True). The `qualifiers` arg is either a mapping or a string.
Always return a mapping if decode is True (and never None).
Raise ValueError on errors.

functions.name: packageurl.normalize
documentation: Return normalized purl components

variables.name: packageurl.basestring
documentation:
A purl (aka. Package URL) implementation as specified at:
https://github.com/package-url/purl-spec
NOQA

classes.name: packageurl.PackageURL

methods.name: packageurl.PackageURL.to_dict
documentation:
Return an ordered dict of purl components as {key: value}.
If `encode` is True, then "qualifiers" are encoded as a normalized
string. Otherwise, qualifiers is a mapping.
You can provide a value for `empty` to be used in place of default None.

methods.name: packageurl.PackageURL.to_string
documentation: Return a purl string built from components.

methods.name: packageurl.PackageURL.from_string
documentation:
Return a PackageURL object parsed from a string.
Raise ValueError on errors.

A purl is a package URL as defined at
https://github.com/package-url/purl-spec

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Rule

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Rule.match
documentation: Match a string with the rule pattern, return True is matching.

A rule is a mapping between a pattern (typically a URI) and a callable
(typically a function).
The pattern is a regex string pattern and must match entirely a string
(typically a URI) for the rule to be considered, i.e. for the endpoint to
be resolved and eventually invoked for a given string (typically a URI).

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.route.RouteAlreadyDefined
documentation: Raised when this route Rule already exists in the route map.

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.route.NoRouteAvailable
documentation: Raised when there are no route available.

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.route.MultipleRoutesDefined
documentation: Raised when there are more than one route possible.

classes.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Router

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Router.__init__
documentation: 'route_map' is an ordered mapping of pattern -> Rule.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Router.append
documentation:
Append a new pattern and endpoint Rule at the end of the map.
Use this as an alternative to the route decorator.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Router.route
documentation:
Decorator to make a callable 'endpoint' routed to one or more patterns.

Example:
\>>> my_router = Router()
\>>> @my_router.route('<http://nexb.com>', '<http://deja.com>')
... def somefunc(uri):
...    pass

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Router.process
documentation:
Given a string (typically a URI), resolve this string to an endpoint
by searching available rules then execute the endpoint callable for
that string passing down all arguments to the endpoint invocation.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Router.resolve
documentation:
Resolve a string: given a string (typically a URI) resolve and
return the best endpoint function for that string.

Ambiguous resolution is not allowed in order to keep things in
check when there are hundreds rules: if multiple routes are
possible for a string (typically a URI), a MultipleRoutesDefined
TypeError is raised.

methods.name: packageurl.contrib.route.Router.is_routable
documentation:
Return True if `string` is routable by this router, e.g. if it
matches any of the route patterns.

A router is:
- a container for a route map, consisting of several rules, stored in an
 ordered dictionary keyed by pattern text
- a way to process a route, i.e. given a string (typically a URI), find the
 correct rule and invoke its callable endpoint
- and a convenience decorator for routed callables (either a function or
 something with a __call__ method)

Multiple routers can co-exist as needed, such as a router to collect,
another to fetch, etc.

functions.name: packageurl.contrib.purl2url.build_repo_download_url
documentation: Return a gitlab download URL from the `purl` string.

functions.name: packageurl.contrib.purl2url.get_repo_download_url
documentation:
Return `download_url` if present in `purl` qualifiers or
if `namespace`, `name` and `version` are present in `purl`
else return None.
